Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Bra009149 is explicitly stated and renamed CYP71CR2. GenBank/TPA accession range BK009360–BK009375 is for CYP71CR genes in Brassicaceae species as a group, not specifically assigned here to CYP71CR2 alone in the main text.

Source_Species.reason: The paper identifies the genes as from Brassica rapa, commonly called Chinese cabbage in the abstract/introduction context.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Subsequent experiments used Rapid Cycling B. rapa, and methods state allele choice was made by comparison with the B. rapa Chiifu reference genome.

Applied_Species.reason: Applied/tested species was Brassica rapa, referred to in the paper as Chinese cabbage/RCBr.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: All subsequent experiments used Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa, with Chiifu-401-42 used as comparison cultivar for metabolite conservation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Explicitly a cytochrome P450 and member of the new CYP71CR subfamily.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Bra009149 was renamed CYP71CR2; it is a cytochrome P450 enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Recombinant enzyme assays directly tested brassinin as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Bra009149-containing microsomes converted brassinin to cyclobrassinin.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports oxidative S-heterocyclization to cyclobrassinin and proposes likely C2,C3 epoxidation followed by sulfur attack at C2 and dehydration.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CYP71CR2 is placed in brassinin-derived indolic phytoalexin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The enzyme specifically forms cyclobrassinin from brassinin, defining the cyclobrassinin branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper states brassinin is a precursor to cyclobrassinin and CYP71CR2 converts brassinin to cyclobrassinin, i.e. a downstream tailoring step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CYP71CR2 directly produces cyclobrassinin in vitro.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports it as a dedicated pathway enzyme/step, but does not test or establish rate-limiting or control-step status.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Cyclobrassinin is the direct product and final linked metabolite for this branch in the study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cyclobrassinin is described as an indolic phytoalexin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Phytoalexins are described as defense metabolites, and cyclobrassinin is stated to be an antineoplastic.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Cyclobrassinin accumulation was measured in elicited leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Direct biochemical evidence shows conversion of brassinin to cyclobrassinin.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in the paper: RNA-seq candidate selection, leaf microsome assay, yeast WAT11 plus ATR1 expression, in vitro NADPH-dependent assays, HPLC-MS/MS, kinetics, deuterium-labeling mechanistic assay, and qRT-PCR/metabolite time course.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RNA-seq identified the gene among CYPs upregulated >4-fold after both flg22 and Psm treatment in B. rapa leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Time-course qRT-PCR/metabolite analyses showed broad agreement between CYP71CR2 transcript and cyclobrassinin metabolite levels in RCBr leaves.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Activity was assayed in microsomal fractions and recombinant yeast microsomes, but endogenous subcellular localization of CYP71CR2 was not directly determined.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The enzyme was co-expressed with CYP reductase ATR1 in yeast for functional assays.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq was performed and deposited under GEO accession GSE69785.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The gene was studied under Pseudomonas syringae pv. maculicola and flg22 elicitation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper discusses that identifying phytoalexin biosynthetic genes would open doors to breeding/metabolic engineering, but this specific application value for CYP71CR2 is prospective rather than directly demonstrated.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Apparent Km 248 ± 23 nM and Vmax 478 ± 15 pmol mg^-1 min^-1 are explicitly given; 6% spirobrassinol side-product for CYP71CR2 is stated; CYP71CR2 is among the first characterized members of the new CYP71CR subfamily.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points are directly supported by Results and Methods.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Bra005870 is explicitly stated and renamed CYP71CR1. The BK009360–BK009375 accession range applies to CYP71CR family sequences collectively, not specifically assigned here to CYP71CR1 alone.

Source_Species.reason: The gene is from Brassica rapa, discussed in the context of Chinese cabbage/crucifers.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The gene was cloned/tested from Rapid Cycling Brassica rapa, and allele selection was by comparison to the Chiifu reference genome.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were performed using Brassica rapa material.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: All subsequent experiments used RCBr, with Chiifu-401-42 used as the comparison cultivar for metabolite profiling.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Explicitly a cytochrome P450 and member of the CYP71CR subfamily.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Bra005870 was renamed CYP71CR1; it is a cytochrome P450 enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Recombinant enzyme assays directly tested brassinin as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Bra005870 produced a distinct product with mass/MS-MS pattern consistent with spirobrassinol.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states CYP71CR1 catalyzes a distinct S-heterocyclization of brassinin to produce a spiro compound likely to be the immediate precursor to spirobrassinin and proposes likely sulfur attack at C3 from a common epoxide intermediate.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CYP71CR1 is linked to brassinin-derived indolic phytoalexin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The enzyme is assigned to the spirobrassinin branch via formation of spirobrassinol.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The enzyme acts downstream of brassinin toward spirobrassinin via spirobrassinol.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper explicitly indicates CYP71CR1 forms a likely immediate precursor, not spirobrassinin itself.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is supported as a dedicated pathway enzyme, but rate-limiting/control status was not tested.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The linked terminal metabolite for this branch is spirobrassinin, while CYP71CR1 forms its likely immediate precursor.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Spirobrassinin is one of the indolic phytoalexins measured in leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: As a phytoalexin, spirobrassinin is part of plant defense; it is also a crucifer dietary metabolite in context of edible crucifers.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Spirobrassinin accumulation was measured in elicited leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Direct biochemical evidence supports formation of spirobrassinol, the likely immediate precursor of spirobrassinin.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RNA-seq candidate selection, yeast WAT11 plus ATR1 expression, in vitro NADPH-dependent assays, LC-MS/MS product analysis, kinetics, and qRT-PCR/metabolite time-course correlation are all described.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states CYP71CR1 and spirobrassinin are both induced late and specifically by Psm elicitation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Time-course data showed transcript abundance broadly agrees with metabolite production, specifically late/Psm-specific induction for CYP71CR1 and spirobrassinin.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Assays used microsomes, but endogenous subcellular localization was not directly established.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The recombinant enzyme was co-expressed with ATR1 in yeast for activity assays.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data were generated and deposited as GSE69785.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The gene was analyzed under Psm and flg22 elicitation; the paper notes the spirobrassinin branch shows late, Psm-specific induction.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper generally suggests pathway genes could aid breeding/engineering, but this specific application for CYP71CR1 is not experimentally demonstrated.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states only trace spirobrassinin formed, implying another enzyme for dehydrogenation; CYP71CR1 has apparent Km <1 µM; and 4% of CYP71CR1 products is cyclobrassinin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each of the four summary statements is consistent with the Results and Methods.
